response.justification: Szabo brings strong technical foundation with 16+ years of software engineering, recent GenAI exposure, and direct MCP experience—all valuable. However, he falls significantly short of the core requirement: 8+ years building production AI solutions. He has approximately 1 year of GenAI/agentic workflow experience (current role). Critically, Python—standard for AI/ML development—is absent from his resume. Vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM optimization, and data pipeline work specific to ML are not demonstrated. While his TypeScript, AWS, and leadership credentials are solid, the gap between his actual AI production experience and the staff-level requirement is too large to overlook.
strengths: Strong TypeScript and backend infrastructure expertise with proven ability to build high-performance systems (Dunnhumby's micro-services handling millions of requests/second)
Direct MCP and agentic workflow experience in current role at re:cinq, showing recent alignment with GenAI stack
Staff-level leadership background with multiple technical leadership roles, mentoring, and workshop/talk contributions demonstrating communication abilities
concerns: Python not mentioned anywhere in resume—a core language for AI/ML development; implies potential ramp-up delay for ML-focused work
Only ~1 year of documented GenAI/agentic experience vs. 8-year requirement; no evidence of vector databases, RAG, LLM performance optimization, or evaluation pipelines for AI systems
AWS and cloud platform experience mentioned only briefly (AWS CDK, Kubernetes); lacks detailed evidence of designing AI systems on major cloud platforms at production scale

key_factors.factor: MCP and agentic workflow hands-on experience
